title: Workspace foundation — JWT claim + ActiveWorkspaceResolver + per-service workspace clamp migration
scope:
Promote workspace from an implicit subscription-equals-workspace assumption to a first-class JWT-clamped dimension. Adds workspace_id to the auth context, introduces an ActiveWorkspaceResolver (single-workspace today, multi-workspace path open), and migrates the two services most affected by chip work — artefacttypes and artefactitems — from subscription-clamped to workspace-clamped queries.

Substrate already in place (per overlap scan): master_record_workspaces table exists (PLA-0032), tenant→workspace inheritance lives (PLA-0051), workspace UUIDs are real backend entities. What's missing: the JWT doesn't carry workspace_id today, and most services scope by subscription_id even when they should scope by workspace_id.

What PLA-0053 builds:

  1. Login flow attaches workspace_id to the JWT alongside subscription_id. Default: the first workspace the user has access to within their subscription.
  2. ActiveWorkspaceResolver Go interface — given a (subscription_id, user_id), returns the active workspace_id. Single-workspace-per-subscription = trivial lookup today; multi-workspace later extends without changing handler signatures.
  3. Migration: artefact_types gains a workspace_id column (NOT NULL, FK to workspaces); existing rows backfilled from their subscription_id via the canonical default workspace per subscription.
  4. artefacttypes service re-scoped: List returns workspace-scoped rows; the subscription-only code path is removed.
  5. artefactitems service re-scoped: WHERE clauses clamp by workspace_id from JWT, not subscription_id.
  6. Frontend useActiveWorkspace() hook surfaces the workspace_id from the JWT claim for client-side cache keying.
  7. Cross-workspace isolation contract tests: integration confirms a user in workspace A cannot read workspace B's artefact_types or artefact_items.

Workspace-aware migration of every other subscription-scoped service is deferred (filed as TD on completion). PLA-0053 only migrates what PLA-0054 depends on.
value:
Without this foundation, PLA-0054's chip rework can't honour the workspace-clamp constraint. Per the user's 2026-05-16 framing: 'workspaces add a further dimension... the overall clamp to everything once tenant is agreed is the workspace itself, all lives inside.'

Three concrete payoffs:

  • Workspace becomes a real, JWT-anchored clamp. Today subscription_id is the de facto boundary; workspace is invisible in code. After PLA-0053, every workspace-scoped service clamps by JWT-derived workspace_id, defending against URL/state tampering at the backend authz layer.
  • Unblocks all chip + catalogue work (PLA-0054, PLA-0055, future per-type catalogue work). The catalogue context can key by workspace; localStorage filter keys can scope per workspace; multi-workspace users get clean isolation.
  • Closes a future migration burden. Multi-workspace ships eventually. Doing workspace-as-clamp today means that future change is mechanical (new workspace switcher UI + JWT refresh on switch), not a multi-week migration of every endpoint.
implementation_plan: RED-GREEN PROTOCOL (HARD RULE): every story below is red-first. For each story: (1) write the failing test described in its AC, (2) commit red with `test(area): red — <story-id>`, (3) implement the change, (4) commit green with `feat|fix|refactor(area): <description> [<story-id>]`. Red commits route through the `<rg>` substrate (PLA-0051) so they show up in Tracker's /red-green dashboard.

TRACKER REGRESSION LIBRARY: every test written by this plan is registered in Tracker under group `backend-workspace-foundation` and becomes a permanent regression check. Future plans' Tracker runs re-execute this group's tests to confirm no regression. Red AND green commits both POST to Tracker (via `<rg>` / `rg-runner`); the green POST closes the cycle and locks the test into the library. The exact `rg-runner` invocation should be verified against `cmd/rg-runner --help` in the MMFFDev - Tracker project before the first story in this plan ships (TD-RG-RUNNER-DOCS if undocumented).
Phase 2 — Service re-scoping: artefacttypes then artefactitems (stories 4, 5).
areas_impacted: SEC: auth.tokens — JWT claim + signing/verification + login response.
API: artefacttypes, artefactitems — service queries clamp by workspace_id.
SQL: artefact_types schema migration + backfill from subscription default workspace.
UI: useActiveWorkspace hook for client-side cache keys (no UI surface change).
feature_list: workspace_id JWT claim on login + auth context
features_extended: artefactitems.Filters — workspace_id clamp added to every query path.
features_removed: Implicit subscription-equals-workspace assumption in artefact_types / artefact_items query paths.

work_item_backlog.title: 00575 — SEC: add workspace_id JWT claim to login + auth context
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish login to attach workspace_id to the JWT alongside subscription_id, so that downstream handlers can clamp by workspace without re-querying.

Login today returns a JWT carrying subscription_id only. The user's active workspace is implicit (subscription = workspace) but the JWT has no claim for it. This story adds workspace_id as a first-class JWT claim and threads it through auth.User. Default workspace = first workspace the user has access to within their subscription.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F1 — Workspace clamp end-to-end. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F1)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F1 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by login response:** POST /v1/api/auth/login returns a JWT whose decoded payload contains both subscription_id and workspace_id fields.
- **As Proven by auth.User struct:** auth.User has a WorkspaceID field populated from the JWT on every authenticated request.
- **As Proven by integration test green:** a user with access to workspace A logs in, JWT carries workspace A's UUID; same user re-logs after admin moves their default to workspace B, JWT carries workspace B's UUID.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00576 — API: ActiveWorkspaceResolver interface + single-workspace implementation
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish a stable ActiveWorkspaceResolver interface, so that future multi-workspace work doesn't change handler signatures.

Introduces an ActiveWorkspaceResolver Go interface: given (subscription_id, user_id), returns the active workspace_id. Today's implementation is the trivial single-workspace-per-subscription lookup. Multi-workspace later extends this without breaking handlers that depend on it.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F1 — Workspace clamp end-to-end. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F1)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F1 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by unit test:** singleWorkspaceResolver returns the user's only workspace_id when called.
- **As Proven by wiring:** auth login uses the resolver to populate the JWT claim; no inline subscription→workspace logic in the auth handler.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00577 — SQL: add workspace_id column to artefact_types + backfill
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish artefact_types to carry a workspace_id FK, so that catalogue reads can be workspace-clamped end-to-end.

Migration adds NOT NULL workspace_id column to artefact_types with FK to workspaces. Backfills existing rows: each row's subscription_id resolves to its default workspace (currently one workspace per subscription).

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F1 — Workspace clamp end-to-end. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F1)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F1 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by post-migration query:** SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artefact_types WHERE workspace_id IS NULL returns 0.
- **As Proven by FK constraint test:** INSERT with a non-existent workspace_id is rejected at the DB layer.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00578 — API: artefacttypes service workspace-scoped (List + GetBySlot)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish artefacttypes.Service to scope by workspace_id, so that catalogue reads honour the workspace clamp.

Replaces the subscription_id WHERE clause in artefacttypes.Service.List with workspace_id. Handler reads workspace_id from auth context (set by PLA-0053 story 1's JWT claim). Removes the legacy subscription-only path.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F1 — Workspace clamp end-to-end. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F1)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F1 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by handler:** GET /_site/artefact-types returns only the rows where workspace_id = JWT workspace_id.
- **As Proven by integration test:** user in workspace A receives only A's artefact_types; the prior subscription-scoped path is gone (compile error if called).

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00579 — API: artefactitems service workspace-scoped (List + Get + filters)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish artefactitems queries to clamp by workspace_id, so that work-items reads honour the workspace boundary.

Replaces the subscription_id clamp in artefactitems.Service queries with workspace_id from auth context. Filters struct passes workspaceID into every SQL builder. Get-by-id still verifies the row belongs to the active workspace.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F1 — Workspace clamp end-to-end. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F1)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F1 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by handler:** GET /_site/work-items returns only artefacts whose artefact_type belongs to the JWT workspace.
- **As Proven by integration test:** a user in workspace A querying a workspace B artefact's ID returns 404, not the row.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00580 — UI: useActiveWorkspace hook + workspace cache-key wiring
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a frontend engineer, I wish a useActiveWorkspace hook, so that client-side caches can be keyed by workspace.

Frontend hook reads the workspace_id from the JWT claim (via existing /me cache or AuthContext) and exposes it as a stable React value. Used by future catalogue providers to key cached data per workspace.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F2 — Frontend workspace awareness. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F2)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F2 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by reactivity:** when AuthContext refreshes with a different workspace_id, components using the hook re-render with the new value.
- **As Proven by null-safety:** before /me resolves, the hook returns null and consumers handle the loading state.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00601 — TEST(F1): workspace clamp end-to-end integration suite
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend/frontend engineer, I wish a single feature-level test suite covering feature F1 (Workspace clamp end-to-end), so that the suite becomes a permanent regression check in Tracker's library and meaningful end-to-end coverage replaces per-story plumbing tests.

**Covers stories:** 00575, 00576, 00577, 00578, 00579.

Single Go integration test suite asserting the workspace clamp works end-to-end. Replaces per-story plumbing tests for the JWT claim + resolver + schema + service re-scoping work in stories 00575–00579.

## Red-Green Protocol (HARD RULE)

1. **RED:** write the failing test suite below. Test must fail on `main` because today's JWT has no workspace_id claim AND artefact_types has no workspace_id column. Commit: `test(workspace): red — F1 workspace clamp [00601]`. Register in Tracker under group `backend-workspace-foundation`, feature `F1`.
2. **GREEN:** stories 00575–00579 land in order; each implementation moves the needle on this suite. The story 00579 green commit must turn this suite green.
3. **REGRESSION LOCK:** test stays in Tracker library; future plans' runs re-execute it.

## Test scope (one suite, multiple assertions)

- POST /v1/api/auth/login as user-A in workspace-A → JWT decoded carries subscription_id AND workspace_id; workspace_id matches workspace A's UUID.
- ActiveWorkspaceResolver.Resolve(subID, userA) returns workspace A's UUID.
- Run migration; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artefact_types WHERE workspace_id IS NULL → 0.
- GET /_site/artefact-types as user-A → only workspace A's rows.
- GET /_site/work-items as user-A → only workspace A's artefacts (rows joined via artefact_type → workspace_id clamp).
- GET /_site/work-items/{workspace-B-item-id} as user-A → 404 (not 403, no existence leak).

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by red commit:** test suite committed and failing on `main`; visible in Tracker dashboard under `backend-workspace-foundation/F1`.
- **As Proven by green commit:** after stories 00575–00579 all merged, the suite passes; Tracker run goes green.
- **As Proven by regression entry:** suite is registered in Tracker's library so future plans (PLA-0056+) re-run it on every push.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00602 — TEST(F2): useActiveWorkspace + per-workspace cache key vitest suite
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend/frontend engineer, I wish a single feature-level test suite covering feature F2 (Frontend workspace awareness), so that the suite becomes a permanent regression check in Tracker's library and meaningful end-to-end coverage replaces per-story plumbing tests.

**Covers stories:** 00580.

Single vitest suite for the frontend workspace-awareness slice.

## Red-Green Protocol (HARD RULE)

1. **RED:** write the failing vitest. Must fail on `main` because useActiveWorkspace doesn't exist yet. Commit: `test(workspace): red — F2 workspace hook [00602]`. Register in Tracker under group `backend-workspace-foundation`, feature `F2`.
2. **GREEN:** story 00580 implements the hook; suite goes green.
3. **REGRESSION LOCK:** suite added to library; runs on every future plan.

## Test scope

- useActiveWorkspace() returns workspace_id from AuthContext.
- AuthContext refresh with new workspace_id → consumer re-renders.
- Hook returns null before /me resolves; consumers handle loading.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by red commit:** vitest committed and failing on `main`.
- **As Proven by green commit:** story 00580 lands; vitest green.
- **As Proven by regression entry:** suite locked into Tracker library.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

user re-login after default-workspace move returns the new workspace_id in the JWT
ActiveWorkspaceResolver interface exported with Resolve(ctx, subID, userID) signature
singleWorkspaceResolver returns the user's only workspace_id
auth login uses the resolver to populate the JWT claim
artefact_types.workspace_id is NOT NULL with FK to workspaces
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artefact_types WHERE workspace_id IS NULL returns 0
INSERT with non-existent workspace_id is rejected at DB layer
artefacttypes.Service.List takes workspaceID uuid.UUID
GET /_site/artefact-types returns only rows where workspace_id = JWT workspace_id
subscription-only path removed (compile error if called)
every artefactitems/sql.go query includes workspace_id = $N predicate
GET /_site/work-items returns only artefacts in JWT workspace
cross-workspace artefact ID returns 404
useActiveWorkspace() returns workspace_id string from AuthContext
components re-render when AuthContext workspace_id changes
hook returns null before /me resolves
workspace-A user sees only workspace A's artefact_types
workspace-A user sees only workspace A's artefact_items

risks.risk: Workspace_id backfill misclassifies a row whose subscription has multiple workspaces (multi-workspace edge case if any exists pre-migration).
risks.mitigation: Pre-migration verification query confirms every subscription has exactly one live workspace; abort + manual triage if any subscription has ≥2.

risks.risk: JWT claim addition breaks downstream services that parse the JWT strictly and reject unknown claims.
risks.mitigation: Audit existing JWT parsers; add the claim as optional with a fallback to subscription_id resolution for one release.

risks.risk: Tests pass with single-workspace fixtures but multi-workspace behaviour is unproven.
risks.mitigation: Story 7 explicitly builds a two-workspace fixture and asserts isolation in both directions.

references.label: Workspace scope is invisible to the user (memory rule)
